evals.name: explicit-slash-command-triggers-and-displays-profile
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, fuzzy-matches 'omar' to omar-zennadi.md (slug-stem-match tier 2 — slug-stem 'omar' equals query). Displays profile block with Name=Omar Zennadi, role/team/relationship from frontmatter, last_interaction surfaced, About paragraph trimmed, Recurring topics bullets, Open commitments counts, source path footer. Read-only — no mutation.
preconditions: Real state on Sid's machine (2026-05-06): beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/omar-zennadi.md exists with frontmatter name=Omar Zennadi, legal_name=Michael Zennadi, role=SVP Engineering, team=AI Task Force.

assertions.text: Output identifies Omar Zennadi as the matched contact (name appears verbatim)
Output references the source path beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/omar-zennadi.md (or token-resolved equivalent)
Output surfaces Omar's role and team (SVP Engineering, AI Task Force)
Output surfaces last_interaction value (or 'no logged interactions yet' if value is 'unknown')
Output does NOT mutate any file under beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/ (read-only invariant)

evals.name: natural-language-entity-recall-triggers-skill
evals.expected_output: Natural-language entity recall pattern. Skill triggers (entity-keyed recall over /find), matches Omar Zennadi, displays profile.

assertions.text: /contact skill triggers (NOT /find), because the query target is a name in contacts/
Output identifies Omar Zennadi as the matched contact
Output does not redirect to /find (precedence: /contact wins for entity-keyed recall)

evals.name: unrelated-prompt-does-not-trigger
evals.expected_output: /contact does NOT trigger. The prompt is not entity-keyed recall about a person in contacts/ — it's a topic question with no contact relevance.

assertions.text: /contact skill does NOT trigger for this prompt
No file under beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/ is read or referenced

evals.name: legal-name-exact-tier-4-finds-omar
evals.expected_output: Tier 4 match: query 'michael' is an exact case-insensitive match against frontmatter legal_name='Michael Zennadi' on omar-zennadi.md (legal_name first-token equals query when normalized — implementation may match either full legal_name or the first whitespace token; either way Omar is the unique tier-4 hit). NOT a multi-match — Michela Fitten is NOT a tier-4 match (her legal_name is unset; her name='Michela Fitten' does not exact-equal 'michael'). Result: single Omar profile.
preconditions: omar-zennadi.md has frontmatter legal_name: Michael Zennadi.
michela-fitten.md has frontmatter name: Michela Fitten and NO legal_name field.

assertions.text: Output matches Omar Zennadi (via legal_name field)
Output does NOT include Michela Fitten as a candidate (her name 'Michela' is not an exact match for 'michael')
Output is a single-profile display (NOT a multi-match disambiguation), because tier 4 produces exactly one hit

evals.name: michela-must-not-substring-match-omar-via-michael
evals.expected_output: Tier 3 (frontmatter `name` exact, case-insensitive) finds 'Michela Fitten' on michela-fitten.md. Critical regression test: Omar Zennadi MUST NOT appear, because (a) tier 4 only does EXACT match on legal_name (and 'michela' is not an exact match for 'Michael Zennadi' or 'Michael'), and (b) tiers 5/6 (prefix/substring) intentionally exclude legal_name from their search surface. So Michela is the unique winner; Omar is not in the candidate set.
preconditions: omar-zennadi.md has legal_name: Michael Zennadi (the regression hazard).

assertions.text: Output matches Michela Fitten (single profile rendered)
Output does NOT mention Omar Zennadi anywhere — not as match, not as candidate, not as suggestion
Output is a single-profile display, NOT a multi-match disambiguation (because tiers 5/6 do not search legal_name as substring)

evals.name: mich-prefix-canonical-single-match-michela-only
evals.expected_output: TIGHTENED canonical reading: no exact tier-1/2/3/4 hits. Tier 5 (prefix on slug or `name`) — per the locked priority order, legal_name is EXCLUDED from tiers 5 and 6 (prefix/substring). The slug `omar-zennadi` does not start with `mich`; the `name` 'Omar Zennadi' does not start with `mich`. The slug `michela-fitten` starts with `mich`, AND the `name` 'Michela Fitten' starts with `mich`. So tier 5 produces exactly ONE hit: Michela Fitten. Single-match Michela rendered. Omar's `legal_name: Michael Zennadi` having prefix `mich` is IRRELEVANT — legal_name is exact-only at tier 4, not in tiers 5/6. This is the locked anti-regression rule from SKILL.md Step 3 lines 76, 80.
preconditions: Both michela-fitten.md and omar-zennadi.md exist; the latter has legal_name: Michael Zennadi (the regression hazard that must NOT match).

assertions.text: Output matches Michela Fitten as a single-profile render (tier 5 prefix on slug 'michela-fitten' and name 'Michela Fitten')

Omar Zennadi MUST NOT appear in the output — not as a match, not as a candidate, not as a suggestion. legal_name 'Michael Zennadi' having prefix 'mich' is excluded from tiers 5/6 by the locked priority order.
Output is a single-profile display, NOT a multi-match AskUserQuestion disambiguation (only one candidate exists under canonical reading)
Implementation does NOT substring-match or prefix-match legal_name in tiers 5/6 — that would re-introduce the regression the priority order explicitly forbids

evals.name: kevin-graceful-no-match-fallback
evals.expected_output: No tier produces any match — Kevin Persaud was removed from contacts/ on 2026-05-06 (per USER.md note). Skill renders the no-match fallback: states no contact named 'kevin', offers /contact-add (planned), offers /find as topic-search alternative, optionally lists ls of contacts/ for reference. NEVER fabricates a profile.
preconditions: No contact file matches 'kevin' across all 6 priority tiers — Kevin Persaud's file was deleted from contacts/ on 2026-05-06.

assertions.text: Output states no contact found / no match for 'kevin'
Output suggests /contact-add (or equivalent scaffold path) as a next step
Output does NOT fabricate a profile, last_interaction date, or any frontmatter field for Kevin
Output does NOT mutate any file under contacts/

evals.name: van-toorn-slug-stem-tier-2-match
evals.expected_output: Tier 2 (slug-stem-match): query 'van toorn' normalizes to 'van-toorn'; the slug 'bob-van-toorn' contains the contiguous stem 'van-toorn'. Single match, profile renders. Demonstrates last-name lookup for multi-word last names.
preconditions: bob-van-toorn.md exists with frontmatter name: Bob van Toorn.

assertions.text: Output matches Bob van Toorn (single profile rendered)
Output references the source path beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/bob-van-toorn.md (or token-resolved equivalent)
Output does NOT prompt for disambiguation — slug-stem-match produces one unique hit

evals.name: contact-wins-over-find-for-entity-keyed-recall
evals.expected_output: Precedence test: 'omar' is a name with a matching contact file (omar-zennadi.md). Even though /find could also handle this query (it greps content + filenames across resources/projects/archive/memory), /contact wins because the target is an entity in contacts/. Skill renders Omar's profile, NOT a /find-style ranked list.

assertions.text: /contact triggers (NOT /find) — output is a contact profile block, not a /find ranked-list with breakdown line
Output identifies Omar Zennadi as the matched contact
Output does NOT include the /find breakdown line ('K resources · L projects · M archive · P memory')

evals.name: al-prefix-genuine-two-way-multi-match-disambiguation
evals.expected_output: Genuine multi-match case (vs `mich` which single-matches under canonical reading). No exact tier-1/2/3/4 hits. Tier 5 (prefix on slug or `name`) produces TWO hits: (a) slug 'alvir-navin' / name 'Alvir Navin' (prefix 'al' on both slug and name), and (b) slug 'alyson-sprague' / name 'Alyson Sprague' (prefix 'al' on both slug and name). No other contact slug starts with 'al' — verified: aden-zaman starts with 'ad'; anthony-livreri / andy-shiels / ankitha-ananda all start with 'an'. Per Step 6, AskUserQuestion fires (multiSelect: false) with these two candidates labeled `<name> — <role>, <team> · <relationship>`. Skill MUST NOT auto-pick either — waits for user input.
preconditions: alvir-navin.md and alyson-sprague.md both exist in contacts/.

No other contact file has slug or `name` with prefix 'al' (verified against the current 20-contact directory: aden-zaman, andy-shiels, ankitha-ananda, anthony-livreri all start with 'a' but not 'al').

assertions.text: Skill fires AskUserQuestion (multiSelect: false) for disambiguation — does NOT auto-pick either candidate
AskUserQuestion is invoked with EXACTLY 2 options: Alvir Navin and Alyson Sprague (no additional candidates)
Each option label includes name + role + relationship (or name + role + team + relationship per SKILL.md Step 6 line 130) — not bare names
Skill does NOT silently pick one match — it stops and waits for user selection
No file under beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/ is mutated (read-only)

evals.name: uppercase-and-mixedcase-queries-match-correctly
evals.expected_output: Case-insensitive matching across the priority order. Per SKILL.md Step 1 line 57 ('Normalize the query: lowercase, trim') and Step 3 (each tier specifies 'case-insensitive'), uppercase 'OMAR' normalizes to 'omar' which then hits tier 2 (slug-stem-match: 'omar' equals slug-token of 'omar-zennadi'). Same single-match outcome as lowercase `/contact omar`. The matched contact is Omar Zennadi regardless of input case. Equivalently `/contact Michela` or `/contact MICHELA` should match Michela Fitten.
preconditions: omar-zennadi.md exists with name: Omar Zennadi.

assertions.text: Skill matches Omar Zennadi (single profile rendered) regardless of input case ('OMAR' vs 'omar' vs 'Omar')
Skill normalizes the query to lowercase before matching (per SKILL.md Step 1 line 57)
Output is a single-profile display, NOT a no-match fallback caused by literal case-sensitive comparison

evals.name: empty-or-whitespace-only-query-asks-for-clarification
evals.expected_output: Empty query (or whitespace-only `/contact   `). Per SKILL.md Step 1 lines 53-55, when the invocation has no name, the skill asks in plain chat: 'Which contact? I'll look across <workspace.root>/<workspace.resources>/contacts/.' Per SKILL.md failure-modes table (line 184) 'Query is empty after normalization' → re-prompt. Critical invariant: NO fuzzy-match attempted on empty input — the skill does not enumerate contacts and try to match against ''; it asks for clarification first. NO contact file is opened or read for matching purposes.

assertions.text: Skill recognizes empty / whitespace-only query and asks the user for a name (plain-chat clarification per Step 1 line 55, or the failure-mode re-prompt per line 184)
Skill does NOT attempt fuzzy-match on empty input (no tier evaluation against empty string)
Skill does NOT open or display any contact file (no profile rendered, no candidate list)
Skill does NOT mutate any file under beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/ (read-only invariant holds even on empty input)

evals.name: subagent-context-multi-match-falls-back-to-inline-list
evals.expected_output: Subagent / worker context where the AskUserQuestion tool is unavailable. Multi-match query 'al' produces two candidates (alvir-navin, alyson-sprague). Per SKILL.md Step 6 last paragraph (line 141: 'If AskUserQuestion is unavailable (subagent context), surface all candidates inline as a list and stop — let the caller re-invoke with a more specific query'), the skill MUST NOT auto-pick either match. Instead it lists both candidates inline (e.g., 'Multiple matches: Alvir Navin (CFO) and Alyson Sprague (VP, MSCI AI Office Hours organizer) — re-invoke with full slug to disambiguate') and stops. Caller is expected to re-invoke with `/contact alvir` or `/contact alyson`.
preconditions: Invocation context is a subagent / worker where the AskUserQuestion tool is not provided.
Both alvir-navin.md and alyson-sprague.md exist (same precondition as fuzzy-al-multi-match-genuine).

assertions.text: Skill does NOT silently pick one match (no Alvir-only or Alyson-only profile rendered)
Skill does NOT attempt to invoke AskUserQuestion in a subagent context (would error)
Skill lists both candidates inline as text (Alvir Navin AND Alyson Sprague both named in output) and stops
Output instructs the caller to re-invoke with a more specific query (e.g., full slug or explicit name)
No file under beru-workspace/3-Resources/contacts/ is mutated
